Review two parallel multiclaude workers on ws-mcp:

1. silly-fox (Phase 2): Implementing MCP JSON-RPC 2.0 server. Worktree: /Users/whit/.multiclaude/wts/ws-mcp/silly-fox, branch: work/silly-fox. Should be creating JSON-RPC dispatch, report.summary handler, wiring into main.go with HTTP+stdio transport, adding tests.

2. brave-raccoon (Phase 3): MCP server config and integration docs. Worktree: /Users/whit/.multiclaude/wts/ws-mcp/brave-raccoon, branch: work/brave-raccoon. Should be creating .mcp.... [truncated, 737 chars total]

primary pass complete. task_id=task-1774584480-f104 task_key=review:step-01:primary analysis_task_id=task-1774586332-2de8 analysis_task_key=review:step-02:interface-contract step=1 risk=cross-worker-interface-contract: brave-raccoon .mcp.json and mcp-serve.sh assume --mode mcp flag that silly-fox has not yet implemented. Also: silly-fox handlers.go missing time import (won't compile). Both workers still active, no commits yet.

CONFIRMED: interface contract mismatch. silly-fox uses --stdio flag, brave-raccoon uses --mode mcp in .mcp.json, mcp-serve.sh, and all README examples. MCP clients will get HTTP server instead of stdio transport. Fix: change brave-raccoon --mode mcp to --stdio (text-only fix). time import issue resolved in committed code. primary_task_id=task-1774584480-f104 primary_task_key=review:step-01:primary analysis_task_id=task-1774586332-2de8 analysis_task_key=review:step-02:interface-contract

CRITICAL: brave-raccoon uses --mode mcp but silly-fox expects --stdio. Fix: change brave-raccoon .mcp.json, mcp-serve.sh, README to use --stdio. Medium: 100-event hard-cap in HandleFilter/HandleSummary. No merge conflicts. 17 tests pass. Both workers on-task. Full findings in .eval-sandbox/review/findings.md
